name: helm-auto-continue
description: Automatically detects chat API errors (503, rate limits, capacity exhaustion) and sends 'Continue' to resume the AI agent.

commands.title: Antigravity Recovery Auto Continue: Show Log
Antigravity Recovery Auto Continue: Report Chat Error
Antigravity Recovery Auto Continue: Simulate Error (Test)
Antigravity Recovery Auto Continue: Mark Window Active (Test)
Antigravity Recovery Auto Continue: Run Full Test

helmAutoContinue.intervalSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to check for chat API errors.

helmAutoContinue.idleTimeoutSeconds.description: If the agent's step index hasn't advanced in this many seconds, assume it silently stopped and send Continue. Good for catching failures that don't produce a log error. Set to roughly how long your longest tool call takes (e.g. 120 for a 2-minute build). 0 = disabled.

helmAutoContinue.startOnActivation.description: Automatically start monitoring when the extension activates.

helmAutoContinue.focusProbe.description: Briefly focus the chat panel on each poll tick to read scoped error context keys, then restore editor focus. Disabled by default because it causes visible chat panel flickering. Only enable if diagnostics-based detection is unavailable and you need context-key error detection.

helmAutoContinue.continuePrompt.description: The message sent to the chat panel when an error is detected. Change this if your AI provider expects a different recovery prompt.

helmAutoContinue.diagnosticsFrequency.description: Run getDiagnostics log parsing every Nth poll tick. Default 1 (every poll) for fastest detection. Higher values reduce overhead but increase detection latency.

helmAutoContinue.postSendCooldownMs.description: Milliseconds to wait after the agent goes idle before sending Continue. This gives the system time to stabilize and avoids sending Continue while the agent is still processing. Default 10s = 2 poll cycles at 5s intervals.

enumDescriptions: Events only — errors, recoveries, continues. Zero output on quiet polls.
State changes, startup info, and dispatch method. Silent during healthy polls.
Full per-strategy diagnostics on every tick (~15 lines/5s). Use for debugging.
helmAutoContinue.logLevel.description: Controls how much detail appears in the Antigravity Recovery Auto Continue output channel.

helmAutoContinue.windowScopeRecovery.description: When enabled, requires that this window's agent was recently active before firing Continue. Prevents cross-window false positives when multiple VS Code windows share the same Antigravity backend — a 503 in Window A won't trigger Continue in Window B if Window B's agent was never busy.

helmAutoContinue.recoveryTimeoutSeconds.description: If the agent in this window has been idle for longer than this many seconds, don't fire Continue even if an error is detected in global logs. Prevents stale error detections from triggering recovery in inactive windows. 0 = disabled.

helmAutoContinue.errorPatterns.description: Regex patterns (case-insensitive) that trigger auto-continue when matched in new log entries. One pattern per entry.

helmAutoContinue.suppressPatterns.description: Regex patterns (case-insensitive) for non-retryable errors. When matched, monitoring stops entirely — retrying would be pointless.

helmAutoContinue.cdpAutoClick.description: Enable CDP Auto Clicker. Requires Antigravity to be launched with --remote-debugging-port=9333. When enabled, automatically clicks Run, Accept, Allow, Apply, Approve, Retry, Continue, and Confirm buttons as they appear in the Antigravity UI.
